summary: Improvement cycle that addresses external reviewer gndrmnn's two NEEDS_CHANGES comments by deleting brittle lexical/keyword-based tests. TestReconcile_DatabaseTLSOrderedBeforeDBConnectionSecret (which read keystone_controller.go source and grepped for substrings) was removed from keystone_controller_test.go along with the now-unused 'os' import. TestBootstrapDBSeedScript_EmbeddedContent (which asserted specific Python keywords inside the go:embed string) was removed from reconcile_bootstrap_test.go; the surviving TestBuildBootstrapJob_PreInsertScript_ParsesSSLDSNParams already proves the embed is wired via NotTo(BeEmpty()) + ContainSubstring(bootstrapDBSeedScript). Two review-pattern catalog files were updated to record the new occurrences, and the external-review JSON was marked ADDRESSED with per-comment resolutions and a resolution_summary.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass for the changed package
No new tests required by the change (deletions only)
Coverage gap from removed tests is justified — embed wiring still proven by surviving behavioral test (reconcile_bootstrap_test.go:730-733)
Sub-reconciler ordering no longer has a unit-level assertion; reviewer explicitly accepted this trade-off because the prior assertion was lexical, not behavioral

code_quality_checklist.item: Surgical scope — only the two test functions named by the reviewer were touched
Unused 'os' import removed from keystone_controller_test.go after the test that referenced it was deleted
No collateral edits to unrelated test cases or production code
go build, go vet, and neighboring tests all clean

security_checklist.item: No security-relevant code changed in this cycle (test deletions only)

architecture_checklist.item: Change is a pure test removal — no production architecture impact
Reconcile sub-reconciler ordering wiring in keystone_controller.go:253 (DatabaseTLS) is unchanged; only the lexical assertion guarding it was removed

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Duplicate / brittle test coverage removed in favour of the surviving behavioral test

suggested_improvements: Optional follow-up: if the project later wants real coverage of the Secrets → DatabaseTLS → DBConnectionSecret ordering (REQ-002), add a behavioral integration test in operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go that drives Reconcile under envtest and asserts the issued cert-manager Certificate exists before the <name>-db-connection Secret is updated — i.e., observe the side effects in order rather than scanning the source. This is a suggestion, not a finding; the reviewer explicitly accepted the gap left by removing the lexical test.
next_steps: Land the change as-is; both reviewer comments are addressed and the external-review JSON is marked ADDRESSED.
Optionally promote the suggested envtest-level ordering check in a future PR if REQ-002 coverage is desired beyond the per-sub-reconciler unit tests already in reconcile_databasetls_test.go.
